engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Brava Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Brava in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Brava 182B2000 engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injection pump failure - high-pressure pump degradation causes hard starting and power loss after 90k miles.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts exhaust flow, triggering emissions faults on urban-driven examples.

cards.altText: Fiat Brava 188A2000 engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant leaks and overheating common after 80k miles on high-mileage units.
Timing belt tensioner wear - belt slip risk if service intervals are missed on interference engine.

cards.altText: Fiat Brava 188A4000 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Balance shaft wear - increased vibration and oil consumption on high-mileage HGT units over 100k miles.
Timing belt failure risk - interference design means belt snap causes catastrophic valve damage if neglected.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £2,000-£3,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 188A4000 units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Brava Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Brava across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Early indirect-injection diesel for the Brava, reliable but low-powered; common on pre-facelift SX/ELX trims.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 TD SX, 1.9 TD ELX, 1.9 TD 100 S
commonFailures: Turbocharger wear on 100bhp variants - whistling noise and power loss after 80k miles
Injector nozzle clogging - rough idle and hard starting on urban-driven examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182B1000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Common-rail JTD 105 replaced the older TD; more powerful and efficient but prone to injection pump issues.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 JTD 105 SX, 1.9 JTD 105 ELX, 1.9 JTD 105 GT
commonFailures: Injection pump failure - high-pressure pump degradation causes hard starting and power loss after 90k miles
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts exhaust flow, triggering emissions faults on urban-driven examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182B2000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Rare 1.6 JTD unit fitted to late-model Bravas; shares architecture with larger JTD but with different tuning.
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure - coolant leaks into exhaust system causing overheating on high-mileage units
Turbo actuator sticking - reduced boost pressure and limp mode on facelift models
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182B3000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 16v petrol for late-model Bravas; economical but underpowered for the car's weight.
commonFailures: Distributor cap wear - misfires and rough running on high-mileage units after 70k miles
Coolant hose degradation - overheating risk if original hoses not replaced on 20+ year old engines
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182B4000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range 12v petrol offering better torque than the 1.2; common on early SX/ELX trims.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear - belt slip risk if service intervals missed on interference engine
Oil seal leakage - external oil leaks from camshaft seals on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 182A3000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Most popular Brava engine; 16v design offered good balance of power and economy but prone to head gasket issues.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant leaks and overheating common after 80k miles on high-mileage units
Timing belt tensioner wear - belt slip risk if service intervals are missed on interference engine
entries.cta: Get quotes for 188A2000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Sporty 16v unit for GT trim; more torque than 1.6 but shares similar failure patterns and maintenance needs.
commonFailures: Valve train wear - tapping noise and reduced performance on high-mileage GT units over 90k miles
Head gasket failure - same weakness as 1.6 16v; overheating can cause catastrophic damage if ignored
entries.cta: Get quotes for 188A3000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Flagship 20v HGT engine; high-revving performance unit with balance shafts, sought after by enthusiasts.
commonFailures: Balance shaft wear - increased vibration and oil consumption on high-mileage HGT units over 100k miles
Timing belt failure risk - interference design means belt snap causes catastrophic valve damage if neglected
entries.cta: Get quotes for 188A4000 Fiat Brava engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Brava Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Brava's 1.6 16v petrol and 1.9 JTD diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. Head gasket failure on petrol units and injection pump issues on diesel are the most common catastrophic faults. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Fiat Brava Head Gasket Failure - Why 1.6 16v Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Brava 1.6 16v SX, ELX, GT (1995-2001, engine codes 188A2000, 182A4000)
problems.rootCause: Original multi-layer steel gasket degrades under thermal cycling; coolant leaks into cylinders causing overheating and compression loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Gasket Replacement - Before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket and bolts, pressure test cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; not recommended once overheating has occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.6 16v engine with upgraded gasket, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for cars worth £1,500+; avoids hidden damage from prior overheating

problems.recommendation: On a Fiat Brava worth £1,000-£2,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better long-term value than a head gasket repair alone, as prior overheating may have warped the head or damaged pistons. We recommend getting quotes for both options before deciding.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 188A2000 engine prices for your Fiat Brava

problems.group: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD Injection Pump Failure
problems.h4: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD Injection Pump Failure - Diesel Starting & Power Loss
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD 105 SX, ELX, GT (1999-2001, engine code 182B2000)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injection pump internal wear reduces fuel delivery pressure, causing hard starting, rough idle and loss of power under load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and refurbish or replace high-pressure pump, recalibrate injectors, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; pump-only fix may not address secondary wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.9 JTD engine with upgraded pump components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines where pump failure indicates broader internal wear

problems.recommendation: If your 1.9 JTD Brava has over 100k miles and shows injection pump symptoms, a full rebuilt engine often provides better reliability than a pump-only repair, as internal wear may be present elsewhere. Get comparative quotes before committing.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 182B2000 Fiat Brava diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Fiat Brava Timing Belt Failure - Interference Engine Catastrophe Risk
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Brava 1.4 12v, 1.6 16v, 1.8 16v, 2.0 20v (1995-2001, all petrol engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-80,000 miles if belt not changed per schedule
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner bearing wear or belt tooth shear causes belt jump/snap; interference design means valves collide with pistons.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt & Tensioner Kit Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys and water pump per service schedule
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; must be done every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after belt failure has caused valve/piston damage; includes new belt kit
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once interference damage has occurred; repair is rarely economical

problems.recommendation: Never skip the timing belt service on a Fiat Brava petrol engine. If the belt has already snapped, a rebuilt engine is almost always more cost-effective than attempting to repair bent valves and damaged pistons on an aged block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt petrol engine prices for your Fiat Brava

problems.group: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD EGR Valve Clogging
problems.h4: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD EGR Valve Clogging - Emissions Faults & Power Loss
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup from exhaust gas recirculation clogs valve and cooler passages, restricting airflow and triggering fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve and cooler, clear fault codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early-stage clogging; may need repeating if driving style unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.9 JTD engine with upgraded EGR components or blanking option, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Consider if EGR issues coincide with other high-mileage wear indicators

problems.recommendation: For urban-driven Bravas with recurring EGR faults, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components or a professionally blanked EGR system can provide a more permanent solution than repeated valve replacements.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 1.9 JTD engines with EGR solutions for your Fiat Brava

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Brava
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing head gasket failure on a 1.6 16v or injection pump issues on a 1.9 JTD, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Fiat Brava specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Brava Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Brava and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Fiat Brava Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Brava is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Fiat Brava 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L JTD diesel was a late addition to the Brava range, offering improved fuel economy over the larger 1.9L units but with less torque; fitted to facelift SX and ELX trims from 1999.
commonFailurePoints: turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage facelift models
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Brava 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Brava 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L diesel was the Brava's workhorse, offered in both indirect-injection TD (pre-1999) and common-rail JTD (1999+) forms; the JTD 105 is the most sought-after diesel replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Injection pump failure on JTD units; turbo wear on 100bhp TD variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Brava 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Brava 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L 16v petrol was introduced with the 1998 facelift as an economical entry-level option; lightweight but often underpowered for the Brava's weight, common on late SX/ELX trims.
commonFailurePoints: Distributor cap wear causing misfires; coolant hose degradation on 20+ year old units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Brava 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Brava 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L 12v petrol was the base engine for early Bravas, offering simple, torquey performance but with known timing belt tensioner wear if service intervals are missed.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt tensioner wear; external oil seal leakage on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Fiat Brava 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Brava 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L 16v was the Brava's most popular petrol engine, balancing power and economy but notorious for head gasket failure on high-mileage units; the most common replacement request.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure after 80k miles; timing belt tensioner wear on neglected units
items.cta: Compare Fiat Brava 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Brava 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 16v powered the sporty GT trim, offering more torque than the 1.6 but sharing similar maintenance needs and head gasket vulnerability on high-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Valve train wear causing tapping noise; head gasket failure if overheating occurs
items.cta: Compare Fiat Brava 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Fiat Brava 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L 20v HGT was the Brava's performance flagship, featuring balance shafts for smoothness and high-revving power; sought after by enthusiasts but requiring specialist rebuild knowledge.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft wear causing vibration; catastrophic valve damage if timing belt fails
items.cta: Compare Fiat Brava 2.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Fiat Brava Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Fiat Brava engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.4 12v petrol to the later 1.9 JTD 105 diesel.

items.title: Fiat Brava Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Bravas (1995-2001) were offered with 1.6L and 1.9L units, with the common-rail 1.9 JTD 105 being the most sought-after replacement due to its balance of power and economy.
items.descriptor: Diesel Bravas (1995-2001) were offered with 1.6L and 1.9L units, with the common-rail 1.9 JTD 105 being the most sought-after replacement due to its balance of power and economy.
items.familiesLabel: Common Fiat Brava Diesel Engine Families
families: 182B2000 - 1.9 JTD 105 (1999-2001), most common diesel replacement, injection pump failure risk
182B1000 - 1.9 TD 75/100 (1995-1999), indirect injection, turbo wear on 100bhp variants
182B3000 - 1.6 JTD (1999-2001), rare facelift unit, EGR cooler failure documented
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and torque for loaded driving
Injection pump failure on JTD units after 90k miles drives replacement demand
EGR valve clogging on urban-driven examples triggers emissions faults
High UK replacement demand on 1.9 JTD 105 ELX/GT variants

engineYears.h2: Fiat Brava Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Brava's 1995-2001 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Brava launched with a range of petrol and indirect-injection diesel engines, establishing the 1.6 16v and 1.9 TD as the volume sellers.
keyChanges: Brava nameplate launched alongside Bravo hatchback on shared 182 platform
Initial engine lineup: 1.4 12v, 1.6 16v, 1.8 16v GT, 2.0 20v HGT petrol; 1.9 TD 75/100 diesel
SX and ELX trim levels introduced with corresponding engine options
knownFor: 1.6 16v quickly became the most popular UK specification
Early 1.9 TD units praised for durability but low power
2.0 HGT gained enthusiast following for high-revving character
Head gasket issues on 1.6/1.8 16v units begin to emerge on high-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-1997 Fiat Brava replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Brava 1.6 16v head gasket replacement • Fiat Brava 1.9 TD turbo repair • Fiat Brava 2.0 HGT timing belt kit
enquiries: Fiat Brava 2.0 HGT timing belt kit

years.description: The 1998 facelift introduced the economical 1.2 16v petrol and the advanced common-rail 1.9 JTD 105 diesel, significantly updating the Brava's powertrain options.
keyChanges: Exterior and interior facelift with revised bumpers, lights and dashboard
New 1.2 16v petrol engine added as entry-level option (1998)
Common-rail 1.9 JTD 105 diesel replaced older TD units (1999)
Introduction of Euro 3 emissions compliance on later models
knownFor: 1.9 JTD 105 became the most sought-after diesel replacement due to power/economy balance
Injection pump failure on JTD units emerges as key replacement driver after 90k miles
1.2 16v praised for economy but often considered underpowered for Brava's weight
Facelift models command slightly higher used values, influencing replacement decisions
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-1999 Fiat Brava replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD injection pump replacement • Fiat Brava 1.2 16v distributor repair • Fiat Brava JTD EGR valve cleaning
enquiries: Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD injection pump replacement
Fiat Brava JTD EGR valve cleaning

years.description: The Brava's final years saw refined versions of existing engines with improved emissions compliance, as production wound down ahead of the Bravo/Brava successor.
keyChanges: Minor trim and equipment updates across SX, ELX and GT ranges
Final Euro 3 compliance updates on diesel engines
Production ended in 2001; remaining stock sold into 2002 registrations
knownFor: Late-model Bravas often better preserved, influencing rebuilt engine demand
1.6 16v and 1.9 JTD 105 remain the dominant replacement requests for 2000-2001 models
EOBD compliance on final models requires compatible replacement engines for MOT
Enthusiast interest in final-year HGT models supports premium rebuild pricing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2001 Fiat Brava replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Brava 2001 1.6 engine replacement • Fiat Brava JTD 105 EOBD compliance • Fiat Brava final year engine compatibility
enquiries: Fiat Brava final year engine compatibility

faq.h2: Fiat Brava Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Fiat Brava engine?
items.answer: Replacing a Fiat Brava engine typically costs £650-£3,000 depending on condition and engine type. Used units start around £650, rebuilt petrol engines average £1,500-£2,200, and performance 2.0 HGT rebuilt units reach £2,000-£3,000.
keyPoints: Used engines: £650-£1,400 supply only, variable mileage and warranty
Rebuilt 1.6 16v petrol: £1,500-£2,200 typical UK market range
Rebuilt 1.9 JTD diesel: £1,800-£2,600 including upgraded injection components
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 to engine price depending on garage location
items.cta: Compare live Fiat Brava engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Fiat Brava?
items.answer: Replacing a Fiat Brava engine is worthwhile if the bodywork is sound and the car has sentimental or practical value. Compare the £1,500-£2,600 rebuilt engine cost against buying another used Brava.
keyPoints: Brava values typically £500-£2,000; engine replacement may exceed car value
Rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty offer better long-term reliability than unknown used units
Consider total ownership cost: tax, insurance, MOT versus replacement vehicle purchase
If bodywork is rust-free and interior good, engine replacement can extend life cost-effectively
items.cta: Get a fixed-price quote to help decide fix or replace for your Brava

items.question: Will a Fiat Bravo engine fit a Fiat Brava?
items.answer: Yes, Fiat Bravo and Brava engines are fully interchangeable as both share the 182 platform and identical engine mounting points across 1995-2001 production.
keyPoints: Bravo and Brava share chassis code 182 and all engine fitments
Engine codes 188A2000, 182B2000 etc. are identical across both models
ECU and wiring loom compatibility confirmed for same-year variants
Always verify engine code matches your Brava's original specification
items.cta: Check Bravo/Brava engine compatibility for your specific variant

items.question: What are the signs of a blown head gasket on a Fiat Brava?
items.answer: Classic Brava head gasket failure signs include mayonnaise-like sludge under the oil cap, white exhaust smoke, coolant loss without visible leaks, and persistent overheating.
keyPoints: Milky residue under oil filler cap indicates coolant-oil mixing
White, sweet-smelling exhaust smoke suggests coolant entering combustion chambers
Coolant level drops without external leaks points to internal gasket breach
Overheating despite full coolant and working thermostat confirms gasket failure
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.6 16v engines with upgraded gaskets for your Brava

items.question: Do Fiat Brava engines use timing belts or chains?
items.answer: All Fiat Brava petrol engines use timing belts except the 1.6 16v which has a chain; diesel 1.9 TD/JTD units use belts. Belt-driven engines require replacement every 60k miles or 5 years.
keyPoints: 1.4 12v, 1.8 16v, 2.0 20v petrol: timing belt, replace every 60k miles/5 years
1.6 16v petrol: timing chain, but tensioner wear can cause rattle and jump
1.9 TD/JTD diesel: timing belt, critical to replace on schedule to avoid interference damage
Always replace water pump and tensioner when changing belt on Brava engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Brava engines with new timing components fitted

items.question: Can I fit a 2.0 20v HGT engine into my 1.6 Brava?
items.answer: Technically yes, but swapping a 2.0 HGT engine into a 1.6 Brava requires extensive modifications including gearbox, wiring loom, ECU and driveshaft changes from a donor HGT.
keyPoints: Engine mounts are similar but not identical; custom brackets often needed
HGT uses different gearbox ratios and clutch assembly than 1.6 models
Wiring loom and ECU must be swapped or extensively reprogrammed for compatibility
MOT emissions testing may fail if ECU doesn't match chassis number records
items.cta: Enquire about specialist Brava engine conversion support from our network

items.question: How long does a Fiat Brava engine replacement take?
items.answer: A typical Fiat Brava engine swap takes 6-10 hours labour at a specialist garage. Allow 2-4 days total for engine delivery, fitting, fluid changes and post-installation testing.
keyPoints: Supply-only engines require you to arrange fitting; allow 1-2 days garage lead time
Supply & fit packages include delivery and installation in one coordinated service
Post-fit road test and ECU adaptation may add half a day to completion time
Delays possible if ancillary parts (hoses, mounts) need replacement during fit
items.cta: Find Fiat Brava supply & fit deals with guaranteed turnaround times

items.question: Do you supply Fiat Brava engines with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt or reconditioned Fiat Brava engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK garage.
keyPoints: Warranty valid nationwide; claim at any VAT-registered garage in the UK
Covers manufacturing defects and premature failure of replaced components
Does not cover damage from incorrect installation, misuse or lack of servicing
Keep all service records and use correct oil specs to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across Fiat Brava engine suppliers in our network

items.question: What oil should I use in a Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD engine?
items.answer: Use 5W-40 fully synthetic ACEA C3 specification oil for Fiat Brava 1.9 JTD engines to protect the turbo and DPF; older non-JTD 1.9 TD units can use 10W-40 semi-synthetic.
keyPoints: JTD 105 engines require low-ash C3 oil to protect emissions equipment
Change oil every 10k miles or 12 months whichever comes first on Brava diesels
Always replace oil filter with genuine or high-quality equivalent at each service
Incorrect oil specification can void engine warranty and damage turbocharger
items.cta: Get quotes for JTD engines pre-filled with correct specification oil

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Fiat Brava?
items.answer: The Fiat Brava engine code is stamped on the cylinder block near the gearbox mounting flange, visible from underneath the car with a torch; also listed on the VIN plate and service book.
keyPoints: Look for alphanumeric code like 188A2000 or 182B2000 on engine block casting
VIN plate under bonnet or driver's door pillar shows engine specification
Service book and MOT certificates often record engine code for reference
Enter your registration on our site to auto-identify the correct engine code
items.cta: Instantly identify your Brava engine code via DVLA lookup on our platform
